concerns.text: Task T020 implements 'GC-matched random genomic regions' as the background model. This contradicts FR-004 in spec.md which explicitly mandates 'using Peak Regions from other cell types as the background model'. The task silently relaxes a functional requirement without a spec amendment or explicit staging note.

Task T031 requires 'ensuring silhouette score ≥0.4 by adjusting clustering parameters if necessary'. This violates the scientific integrity of the acceptance criterion in US-3 (spec.md) which requires the score to be a *result* of the data clustering, not a forced metric. Forcing parameters to meet a threshold constitutes data manipulation and weakens the validation claim.

Task T005 checks for '≥14GB free space'. FR-002 and US-1 require a pre-flight check for '≥14GB available space' but the Plan (plan.md) explicitly states the runner has only '~14GB total disk'. A task checking for 14GB *free* space on a 14GB *total* disk is a semantic weakening of the feasibility constraint; it will always fail or require a spec change to lower the threshold to match the hardware reality.

Task T025 implements 'chunked processing' to stay under 7GB RAM. While the Plan mentions this, FR-002 and SC-004 in the spec assume 'in-memory processing' or do not explicitly authorize chunking as a fallback. Implementing chunking without a corresponding spec amendment or explicit 'Plan Correction' tag in the task description risks violating the 'Single Source of Truth' principle if the spec implies a different memory model.
